questions.q: In which present-day Indian state does the plain of Kurukshetra lie?
questions.explanation: Kurukshetra is in modern Haryana, where the town of the same name still draws pilgrims.

questions.q: By what name does the Bhagavad Gita's very first word describe Kurukshetra?
options: Devakshetra, the field of the gods
questions.explanation: The Gita opens with the words dharmakshetre kurukshetre, naming it the field of dharma before any warrior.

questions.q: After which king is Kurukshetra named?
questions.explanation: The field bears the name of King Kuru, ancestor of both the Kauravas and the Pandavas.

questions.q: For how many days did the great war rage at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: The war lasted eighteen days, from Bhishma's ten-day command to the final mace duel.

questions.q: How many akshauhini armies assembled on the field of Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Eighteen akshauhinis gathered there, seven for the Pandavas and eleven for the Kauravas.

questions.q: What did King Kuru famously do to sanctify this land?
options: Ploughed the field with his own hands
Built a thousand fire altars upon it
Bathed it in waters carried from the Ganga
Planted a forest of sacred fig trees
questions.explanation: King Kuru tilled the field himself for years, an act of devotion that hallowed the ground forever.

questions.q: Which celebrated discourse was spoken on this field just before the fighting began?
questions.explanation: Krishna delivered the Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna between the two armies drawn up at Kurukshetra.

questions.q: Which warrior lay for weeks upon a bed of arrows on the field of Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: After falling on the tenth day, Bhishma rested on the shafts that pierced him, choosing the hour of his death.

questions.q: Who created the five lakes of blood associated with Kurukshetra in an earlier age?
questions.explanation: Parashurama filled five lakes with the blood of his slaughtered enemies at this very place.

questions.q: What is the name of the site of those five lakes within Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Samantapanchaka, the place of the five lakes, is the older sacred name for the battleground.

questions.q: With whose blood did Parashurama fill the five lakes?
options: Demons slain to avenge his father
The rakshasas of the northern forests
The kin of Kartavirya Arjuna alone
questions.explanation: Parashurama cleansed the earth of kshatriyas twenty-one times and pooled their blood at Samantapanchaka.

questions.q: What boon did King Kuru win for those connected with this field?
options: Rebirth in a royal line for all pilgrims
Freedom from every curse upon the land
A painless and instant death for the old
Passage to heaven for those who die there in battle or penance
questions.explanation: Kuru's austerity won the promise that whoever dies at Kurukshetra fighting or performing penance attains heaven.

questions.q: Which god granted Kuru the boon that sanctified the field?
questions.explanation: Indra, after questioning the ploughing king, granted that those who die there go to heaven.

questions.q: What did King Kuru say he was sowing as he ploughed the field?
options: Seeds of golden grain for the gods
The eight virtues, beginning with truth and austerity
The ashes of his royal ancestors
Seeds of the celestial kalpa tree
questions.explanation: Kuru declared he was cultivating austerity, truth, forgiveness, kindness, purity, charity, yoga, and continence.

questions.q: In which lake on the Kurukshetra plain did Duryodhana hide after his armies fell?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana cooled his wounds beneath the waters of Lake Dvaipayana until taunts drew him out to the duel.

questions.q: Balarama's wartime pilgrimage along which river ended at Kurukshetra just before the mace duel?
questions.explanation: Refusing to take sides, Balarama toured the holy fords of the Saraswati and arrived in time to watch the final duel.

questions.q: Between which two rivers does tradition place the holy land of Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: The epic locates the sacred region between the Saraswati to the north and the Drishadvati to the south.

questions.q: Beside which river did the Pandavas pitch their camp at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: The Pandava host encamped on the banks of the Hiranvati after marching to the field of war.

questions.q: What did Gandhari do when she surveyed the corpse-strewn field with divine sight after the war?
options: She forgave the Pandavas at Vyasa's plea
She vowed to fast unto death upon the field
She cursed Krishna and the Yadava race
She fell senseless and never spoke again
questions.explanation: Beholding her hundred sons dead on Kurukshetra, Gandhari cursed Krishna's clan to destroy itself in thirty-six years.

questions.q: For what celestial turning did Bhishma wait upon his arrow bed at Kurukshetra?
options: The full moon of the Kartika month
The autumn equinox of the gods
The dawning of the new yuga
questions.explanation: Empowered to choose his hour of death, Bhishma waited for the auspicious northern journey of the sun.

questions.q: The epic exalts Samantapanchaka at Kurukshetra as the sacrificial altar of whom?
options: Brahma, the creator, as his northern altar
questions.explanation: Kurukshetra is praised as the northern sacrificial altar of Brahma, making the war itself a kind of sacrifice.

questions.q: The war at Kurukshetra is said to have been fought at the junction of which two yugas?
questions.explanation: The epic places the war at the twilight where the Dwapara Yuga gives way to the Kali Yuga.

questions.q: King Kuru, who gave the field its name, was the son of which royal pair?
questions.explanation: Kuru was born to King Samvarana and Tapati, daughter of the sun god, as the Adi Parva relates.

questions.q: How many nights did Bhishma say he had lain upon the bed of arrows at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Bhishma tells Yudhishthira he has lain fifty-eight nights on the sharp arrows awaiting the sun's turn.

questions.q: What rite did Parashurama perform with the blood of the five lakes?
options: A victory sacrifice offered to Shiva
Tarpana, libations offered to his ancestors
An atonement for the kings he had slain
The consecration of his battle axe
questions.explanation: Parashurama offered the blood as libations to his forefathers, who then granted him pardon for the slaughter.

questions.q: Who utters the words dharmakshetre kurukshetre that open the Bhagavad Gita?
questions.explanation: The blind king Dhritarashtra speaks the Gita's first verse, asking Sanjaya what happened on the holy field.

questions.q: What does the name Samantapanchaka literally evoke?
options: The five lakes lying all around
The fifth altar of the gods
Five rivers meeting in one plain
The five sons of King Kuru
questions.explanation: The name refers to the five pools of Parashurama spread across that sacred region.

questions.q: After the war, where were the water libations for the slain of Kurukshetra offered?
options: At the five lakes of Samantapanchaka
On the banks of the Ganga
At the source of the Saraswati
In the Hiranvati beside the camp
questions.explanation: The mourners went to the Ganga for the water rites, where Kunti revealed that Karna was her firstborn son.

questions.q: According to the epic, what marked the holy boundary of the Kurukshetra region?
options: A ring of one hundred fire altars
The shadow of the Himalaya at dawn
The points Tarantuka, Arantuka, Machakruka, and Rama's lakes
Pillars raised by King Bharata's victory rites
questions.explanation: The Vana Parva bounds Kurukshetra by Tarantuka, Arantuka, Machakruka, and the lakes of Rama, calling all within it holy.

questions.q: Duryodhana's refuge, Lake Dvaipayana, shares its name with which figure of the epic?
options: Shuka, the enlightened son of Vyasa
Vyasa, called Krishna Dvaipayana, the island-born
questions.explanation: Dvaipayana, meaning island-born, is Vyasa's own epithet, and the lake of Duryodhana's hiding bears the same name.
